name: the primitive type called fee

VINIT.justification: the state before any temporal orientation or chiral bias is introduced

TANCH.justification: the boundary that prevents the accumulation of temporal memory

AFWD.justification: a single transition forward in time without memory of the previous state

AREV.justification: a single transition backward in time that is indistinguishable from the forward step

CLINK.justification: the chaining of instantaneous states into a sequence

IMSCRIB.justification: the recognition of the current state as independent of its history

FSPLIT.justification: the branching of a path into potential forward and backward vectors

FFUSE.justification: the reunification of directions into a single achiral point

EVALT.justification: the confirmation that the forward path maintains symmetry

EVALF.justification: the detection of a memory-induced bias in the transition

ENGAGR.justification: the state where forward and backward directions are held as identical

IFIX.justification: the permanent log of the deletion of temporal history

phase_3.false_description: a broken symmetry or memory leak
phase_3.both_description: a state of perfect achiral indifference

steps.domain_action: initialize the system in a state of zero temporal bias
identify the current moment as the sole operational frame
branch the current state into potential forward and backward morphisms
validate the forward morphism as a memoryless transition
execute the backward step on the opposing branch
check for the presence of temporal memory or chirality
fuse the branches to confirm the identity of the forward and backward paths
compose the result into a continuous but memoryless sequence
hold the forward and backward vectors in a state of simultaneous indifference
record the successful erasure of temporal history to maintain the fee state
close the system within the achiral boundary

phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the erasure of memory offsets the gain from state transition
